whatThisIs: Walk through the federal No Surprises Act Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) eligibility checks for a balance bill or out-of-network payment dispute. Sophie Well steps through the threshold questions - is the service in scope (emergency, post-stabilization, or out-of-network at an in-network facility), has the 30-business-day open-negotiation period elapsed, is the dispute within the 4-business-day initiation window after negotiation, and is the patient or plan eligible to initiate - and returns a plain-language go / no-go with the governing 45 CFR 149.510 reference.
whenToUse: Use this when a provider, plan, or patient is weighing whether to spend the IDR administrative fee on a balance-bill dispute and needs to confirm the dispute even qualifies before paying. The checker is a triage, not legal advice - state IDR programs (Texas, Georgia, California) preempt the federal program for state-regulated plans, and ground ambulance balance bills are excluded from federal IDR entirely.
